channel.title: feedback on Jens Rantil
channel.description: Recent content in feedback on Jens Rantil

item.title: The hidden opportunity in feedback
item.description: Today I would like to talk about a missed opportunity when giving and receiving feedback – mainly the discussion that happens afterwards. A classical way to give feedback is using the observed-perceived method: Describe the observed behavior from your point of view. Do it as soon as possible about the specific behavior that occurs. Describe the perceived impact of the observed behavior. Unfortunately, what commonly follows is that the receiver of the feedback explains why the observed behavior was so – followed by ending the feedback session.
